title: Full-auto fire rate gated on per-rendered-frame counter (field_88C), unscaled — automatics likely fire 2-4x too fast at locked 60Hz vs N64 15-30Hz
repro: input tape holds fire; count shots per wall-second in ares oracle (real N64 frame costs) vs native (g_ClockTimer==1 at 60Hz); field_88C++ unscaled while adjacent field_890 += g_ClockTimer is tick-scaled; gate is field_88C % fire_rate

history.note: Survey F1. GoldenRecomp README confirms empirically (unfixed). Mechanism confirmed in our source. Needs ares cadence A/B to quantify, then fidelity-policy call (retail-code-at-60Hz vs retail-experience-at-15-30Hz); remediation design in survey F18 (scale per-frame counters by g_ClockTimer behind Input.FireRateAuthentic). Oracle lane buildable now that combat oracle FID-0032 is verified.

Quantified: AK47 sustained full-auto on Dam fires 33.3 shots/100 ticks at locked 60Hz (SPEEDFRAMES=1) vs 11.3 at the N64 ~20fps frame cost (SPEEDFRAMES=3) = 2.95x overspeed. Mechanism confirmed: gun.c field_88C advances per-frame (60Hz), gate field_88C%fire_rate; N64 frame cost g_ClockTimer=2-4.

Fixed behind Input.FireRateAuthentic (opt-in, default OFF; core-combat-feel owner policy). fireRateCounterAdvance tick-scales field_88C by g_ClockTimer; fireRateEffectiveAutoRate multiplies the automatic divisor by Input.FireRateN64FrameCost (default 3). A/B at locked 60Hz: OFF 33.3, ON 11.0 shots/100 ticks (matches N64-truth 11.3). Byte-identical under default (Release combat smoke hash 3c8939968e0eb50e; AK47 scenario d06b1c7a on fixed-OFF==main). ctest fire_rate_authentic PASS + proven fail-on-revert (8 assertions red, ratio 1.00x). Not verified: needs full ares A/B in verify_all.
